title: Improve flow security with flow-based access control
html:
While some permissions can be managed in the flow Settings under Account administration, you can achieve more granular control through the Security settings of each individual flow.

How it works

In the Security settings of a specific flow, you can choose between Global permissions and Customized permissions based on your access needs.

Setting Global Permissions

When enabled, global permissions allow all flows across your account to access the selected flow. You can specify whether all fields should be accessible or limit access to only certain fields.

Adding Customized Permissions

With customized permissions, you can define access levels for individual flows. This lets you:

  • Grant specific permissions to a process, board, or dataset.
  • Control whether all data or only specific fields are accessible for each flow

For example, to share the Attendance process with another flow, such as Access requests, follow these steps:

  1. Open the Attendance process.
  2. Navigate to Manage > Security > Security settings.
  3. Click + Add a customized permission and select the Access requests board.
  4. In addition, you can also limit the fields that the shared board can access.

This ensures controlled data sharing while maintaining security and flexibility.

Note

You can enable both global and customized permissions at the same time.

Read more about flow-based access control here.

title: Common errors to avoid when computing your Kissflow Form fields
html:
When working with formulas in Kissflow form fields, errors in computation are a common challenge. This guide highlights common mistakes and provides practical tips to ensure accurate calculations.

Using functions instead of field names

Some form fields may have names like Currency, Date, or Number—which are also function names in Kissflow. This can lead to confusion when writing formulas.

To differentiate between functions and field names, remember:

  • Functions are written as: currency(), number(), date()

  • Field names appear as plain text without parentheses.

Text casing and spacing

Formulas are sensitive to case and spacing. For example, if a formula expects the value "NA", but the actual value retrieved is "Not applicable", the formula will fail due to the mismatch. Always ensure consistency in text formatting.

Incorrect Field Type and Expression

Using an incorrect expression for a field type can cause errors.

 

For example:

  • Trying to compute a Number field using a Text expression.
  • Applying a Date & Time expression to a Date field.

Please ensure the expression's return type matches the field type to avoid mismatches.

By being mindful of these common pitfalls, you can create formulas that compute fields accurately and without issues.

Have you encountered formula errors in Kissflow? Share your experience and solutions in the comments to help fellow community members!

title: Coming soon: Custom form field component for Kissflow Apps
html:
We are excited to announce that platform extension capabilities will soon be available in Kissflow, giving you the power to customize features and tailor the platform to your unique needs.

As a first step, we are introducing the new custom field component feature that will allow you to add custom form fields to use across all forms within an app. This will include a specialized SDK bundle to help you understand how custom form fields function.

This release will introduce custom field components and enhanced page components, with future expansions planned to extend these capabilities across the platform.

What’s changing?

Currently, only one type of custom component can be created. With this release, you’ll be able to choose between:

  • Page components – Custom components designed for pages.

  • Field components – Custom form fields to use in forms.

 

You can manage both types of custom components from a unified Manage components page.

 

What this means for you

  • When creating a new component, you will choose either Field or Page to define the custom component’s type.

  • After this release, the Manage components page will be the centralized space where you can view, sort, and archive all your custom components. This means that the custom component management options will be removed from the left navigation panel in the app builder.

  • The form builder will feature a new category of fields called Custom where all the published custom form fields will be listed for use.

  • There will be no changes in how page components behave and how you use them.

 

This feature will roll out in April 2025 exclusively to Enterprise customers.

Stay tuned for more updates on this release, and feel free to leave a comment if you have any questions!

title: Product Tip: How to deactivate an inactive user or admin from your Kisslow account
html:
Follow the below steps to deactivate an inactive user or admin from your account:

You need to have Admin access to deactivate a user from the account. 

Click on your profile picture > Account administration > User management > Manage users > Select the checkbox beside the user and then click Deactivate. Once a user is deactivated, the count is automatically updated in the Inactive users tile. Inactive users are not allowed to sign in until their license is reactivated.

 Help docs: https://helpdocs.kissflow.com/user-management/add-manage-users#deactivating_and_activating_users

title: Set Daily Reminders With the Scheduler Connector in Kissflow Integrations
html:
The scheduler is a built-in Kissflow connector that schedules an integration trigger run at a specific interval. Integration runs are typically only executed when a trigger event occurs in a connector. You can use scheduler triggers every day, weekly, monthly, or yearly.

 

To set up the Scheduler connector, 

  • Go to My profile > Integrations > +New integration and create a new integration. 

  • Select Schedule by Kissflow under +Add a trigger and choose to schedule daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly. 

To schedule,

  • Daily - Choose the exact hour for each occurrence.

  • Weekly - Choose specific days of the week and specify the hour of the day for each occurrence.

  • Monthly - Choose a start date, the day of the month, and specify the hour of the day for each occurrence.

  • Yearly - Choose a start date, the month, and the day of the month, and specify the hour of the day for each occurrence.

How does it work? 
Sarah is responsible for approving vendor invoices for translation in the finance team. As she deals with multiple vendors, she handles multiple items for approval daily, sometimes leading to delays in timely approval. Sarah has set up a reminder using the scheduler connector to ensure she doesn't miss out on invoice approvals. This way, she receives email reminders for all pending approvals until she approves them.

https://www.loom.com/share/2c04140b8d804cd5b53b69cf53714365?sid=0dcbdb29-1625-4491-96c9-18b42037e9f4

Pro Tip:
The time you choose is associated with the time zone you selected in My settings > Format settings. If you have not selected a local time zone setting, it will default to the Admin time zone settings. 

Read more about the Scheduler connector here. 

 proposed this valuable use case to streamline the manual reminder process for multiple approvers. Adopting this solution has saved time by automating reminders, and reducing the need for manual follow-ups.

title: Product Tip: How to fetch the approver of a workflow step in your Kissflow process report
html:
In a process form, you can find the name of the approver for a workflow step under the status section. Now, if you want the approver’s name in a process report, you can achieve this by editing the report and adding the step details as a field. Now let’s see how you can do this.

  • Click on Reports of the process you’re working on and open the report you’d like to edit. 
  • Click Edit > + Add fields under Settings. 

Under Workflow, select the steps you’d like to add to your report and click Save.

The workflow steps will now be included as columns in your report. You can find the approver of the step (ActedBy), when the action was performed (ActedAt), along with the status of the item (Status), and whether it was completed before the deadline or not (SLABreached). For approver time you need to create a report using Kissflow analytics.
 

Do you have any questions? Drop them in the comments and we’ll help you out. 

Do react to this tip if you found this helpful :)

title: Automating Contract Creation With Conditional Logic in Kissflow Document Templates
html:
Do you want to automatically retrieve specific form field data and customize document templates based on conditions?
Using a straightforward if-else condition can dynamically fetch values in your document template depending on the conditions specified.

How does it work? 
Stark Fitness Centre offers different membership options, each with unique terms and conditions. To streamline contract creation, they've integrated Kissflow. This system generates personalized PDF contracts based on the chosen membership plan, inserting pricing and benefits dynamically into the template. For instance, basic memberships include three free extra months, while premium memberships come with six additional months at no cost.

Here's the formula they have used in the document template:
{% if Membership_Type == 'Basic' %} 3 months of subscription for free.  {% elif Membership_Type == 'Premium' %} 6 months of subscription for free.  {% endif %}

https://www.loom.com/share/89df7893958643cf968998ccdab931fd?sid=8ef3358a-458a-4ea2-ab13-64e3485b878a

Pro Tip: The attachment size cannot exceed 10 MB while sending an attachment via the email connector.

Learn more about creating document templates here. 

 suggested using this use case to automate customization for numerous document templates with simple logical conditions, which has saved time and manual effort.

title: Product Tip: How to configure a Yes/No field in a Kissflow form
html:
Setting a default value for a Yes/No field

  1. Drag and drop a Yes/No field into the form.
  2. To set the default value as Yes or No, change the toggle as required in the Settings panel and save.

Note: In a process form, you can click Simulate to check if the changes are reflected before making the form live.

Setting validation rules for a Yes/No field

You can set validation rules for the Yes/No field to change the way it behaves based on your condition. Here are the types of validation rules that can be used. Yes/No field cannot be used as a computed field, i.e., you cannot set a custom formula for a Yes/No field. However, this field can be used in combination with another computed field if required.

Example scenario

You can pass the value of a Yes/No field to a text field conditionally. To do this,

  1. Set Field 1 as a Yes/No field and Field 2 as a text field.
  2. Change Field 2 into a computed field and use this formula in the formula builder: If(Field_1 = true(), "YES", "NO").
  3. Now, Field 2 will display the value of the Yes/No field.

title: 🚀 Introducing board-to-process connection! 🎉
html:
We’re thrilled to announce the expansion of connected flows to bring you a powerful new capability - Board-to-process connection.

Previously, you could make board-to-board and process-to-process connections. Now, you can seamlessly connect a board to a process and automate item creation between them.

For example, when an onboarding task reaches a certain status in your Employee onboarding board, an IT asset request process item can be triggered automatically and instantly. No manual handoffs needed!

This update enhances workflow automation, reduces manual effort, and enhances synergy between your boards and processes. Check out the help documentation and try it out today!

title: Introducing the all-new app deployment experience
html:
We are excited to announce a completely redesigned app deployment flow, crafted to make the app deployment process faster, more transparent, and easier than ever.

What’s new?

The revamped deployment process comes with meticulous pre-deployment validation steps that verify the data in the app and checks for unresolved dependencies before deployment:

  • Flow validation: Validates both newly added flows and existing ones if they are modified.

  • Integration actions: Verifies the integrations added in a particular deployment.

  • Dependencies with other apps: Verifies if data consumed from other apps is published and ensures there are no cross-app dependencies.

  • Data type validation: Validates whether the form field data types perfectly match the entered information, preventing potential runtime errors.

What this means for you

  • Faster deployments: The enhanced deployment process cuts deployment time by up to 50%, thanks to comprehensive validation checks and actionable error resolutions that streamline the workflow.

  • Greater transparency: Gain better visibility into your deployment process with detailed previews of changes before deployment. The updated deployment history page provides a clear record of overall changes and error logs for failed deployments, giving you better insights for greater control.

  • Intuitive user interface: The redesigned deployment window comes with a sleek and intuitive interface that makes it easy to navigate through action items, access essential information, and find support when needed.

With this release, we are making deployments simpler, more transparent, and actionable. We hope to empower you to identify and troubleshoot deployment issues independently and reduce the reliance on engineering teams.

Try out the new deployment process and let us know what you think! For more information, check out the help documentation.

title: Introducing: Enhanced progress bars
html:
In addition to our linear progress bar, we're thrilled to announce that we have expanded our visualization options for the progress bar.

What’s new?

We are introducing two new types of progress bars: Circular and Semicircular.

  • Circular: A complete circular design that provides a compact and visually appealing progress indicator.
  • Semicircular: A curved, half-circle representation of progress.

On-click event capabilities

The progress bar component now supports configurable on-click events with the following capabilities:

  • Redirection: Enables navigation to a specific page in the app or return to the current page.

  • Open popup: Opens a popup created using the Popup component, providing quick access to additional context or information.

  • JavaScript action: Provides the flexibility to define custom actions using JavaScript, allowing you to implement complex interactions and logic specific to your app’s requirements.

How it helps

These enhancements provide more design flexibility and offer a compact visualization for mobile and constrained interfaces, enabling developers to represent progress more dynamically and add interactive elements through configurable events.

Example: In a project management dashboard, clicking a progress bar could redirect to a page that has additional task details or return a variable via the custom JavaScript code.

Give it a try, and let us know what you think. For more information, visit our help documentation.

title: Introducing connected flows inside process management and governance
html:
We’re delighted to introduce a dedicated Connected flows section to streamline how you manage and oversee your workflows.

Inside Associated flows widget

As a Process Admin, you’ll now find a new Connected flows section that lets you see all the processes connected with your process. Head to Associated flows under the Managed by me page to access it and make use of the following two sections:

 

Dependent flows - View the connected processes that can automatically initiate items from your current process' items.

Source flows - View the connected processes that can automatically initiate items in your current process.

 

This addition gives you quick and easy access to all the processes linked to the processes you manage, giving you more control.

Inside Account governance

Super Admins can now oversee all connected workflows directly from the Account governance menu. With this new capability, you gain complete visibility into all the connected processes, making it simpler to audit, monitor, and manage organizational workflows.
 

These updates are designed to improve efficiency, and give you a holistic view of your workflows. Explore the Connected flows section today and take your process management to the next level!

 

Do try this feature and let us know your feedback in the comments below.

title: New add-on: Query multiple parameters simultaneously
html:
We’re excited to introduce an enhancement to filter parameters in the page builder. Until now, when you added a view or report to a page, all associated parameters appeared in the Filter Parameters section, allowing you to type in or assign a single filter parameter to refine the view or report.

What’s new?

Now you can use up to 10 parameter values, be it self-defined, or local or global variables, to refine your view or report. Simply separate all values with commas, regardless of their nesting properties, and you are good to go.

This enhancement is only applicable when the advanced filter’s configuration contains the following operators: Part of, Not part of, Contains, Not contains, and In. For all other operators, the behavior remains as it was previously.

How it helps

Imagine you’re creating an employee attendance report. With the new filter parameter feature, you can refine the data to show attendance percentages based on selected departments. 

Enter the department names, separated by commas.

For example: HR, Marketing, Sales

 

This feature helps app developers to create more targeted views or reports with ease. Give it a try and let us know your feedback. For more information, visit our help documentation.

title: Enhancements to board views
html:
We’re excited to announce some powerful updates to board views that take your Kissflow experience to a next level. These features are designed to make your workflows more dynamic, collaborative, and tailored to your needs, all while adding a touch of sophistication to your boards. Here’s what’s new:

 

 

Expanded view sharing

View sharing is more flexible than ever. You can share a view with individual board users as well as account users and groups who are not part of the board. You can make them viewers or members for each view. This flexibility allows you to bring the right people into the loop, whether they’re part of the board or not.

Inside apps, you can share views with app roles as well. 

 

Show/hide fields on item cards and layouts

You have the ability to control which fields are displayed in the layout for each view.

This functionality is available for both boards inside and outside apps, giving you more control over how your data is displayed.

Field permissions inside board views

Take control over how users interact with various item fields in a particular view by setting permissions for fields, tables, and sections in the item form. You can set the below permissions:

 

Editable - The field in the selected view can be edited.

Read-only - The field can only be viewed and not edited in the selected view.

Hidden - The field will be hidden and cannot be viewed or edited.

 

New Edit access permission 

We’re introducing a new Edit access permission while sharing a board inside apps for more flexibility and control.

 

 

Revamped view configuration panel

The View configuration panel is getting a fresh look with a more streamlined design, making it easier to manage and customize your views.

 

These enhancements are designed to give you more control, flexibility, and ease of use when working with board views. Check out our help documentation to learn more on board views.

Do try this feature and let us know your feedback in the comments below.

title: Upcoming changes to the app deployment process
html:
We are excited to announce some upcoming changes that would simplify and streamline the app deployment process for you.

What’s changing?

The app deployment revamp aims to deliver a smoother deployment experience through comprehensive validations to verify the data and check for unresolved dependencies before deployment. This includes identifying data type mismatches, dependencies with other apps, and flow validations.

What this means for you

Currently, the pre-deployment validation window provides only basic details such as the build number and target environment. This release will offer transparency on which flows are going into the build and give you greater control over the overall deployment process.

In addition, you will be notified about new and modified integrations post-deployment so that you can reauthenticate and restore their functionality. A link to the list of integrations needing your attention will be provided on the deployment success page and shared via notifications.

 

This release is slated to roll out in the second week of January 2025. If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to leave a comment or reach out to our support team. Stay tuned for the official launch!
